vocab.meaning: một cảnh tượng mãn nhãn/đẹp mắt
vocab.example: I wouldn't say my reflection is a feast for the eyes, but I do check the mirror to ensure my outfit is coordinated.

vocab.meaning: là người rất chú trọng vẻ bề ngoài (nói vui)
vocab.example: Admittedly, vanity is my middle name, so I probably glance at my reflection more often than the average person.

vocab.word: look like a sight for sore eyes
vocab.ipa: /lʊk laɪk ə saɪt fə sɔːr aɪz/
vocab.meaning: trông rất dễ chịu/vui mừng khi thấy (thường dùng sau khi vừa tút tát lại)
vocab.example: After a long day at the office, seeing myself finally cleaned up in the mirror makes me feel like a sight for sore eyes.

vocab.meaning: chấp nhận mọi khuyết điểm/toàn bộ sự thật
vocab.example: As I’ve grown older, I’ve learned to appreciate what I see in the mirror, warts and all, without being too critical.

vocab.meaning: tóm lại là (dùng để chuyển ý khi kể về thói quen soi gương)
vocab.example: I used to be obsessed with my hair but, to cut a long story short, I now only look in the mirror twice a day.

vocab.meaning: gọn gàng, sạch sẽ, ngăn nắp
vocab.example: I mainly use the mirror to ensure that my grooming is spick and span before I head out for a formal event.

vocab.word: give someone a run for their money
vocab.ipa: /ɡɪv ˈsʌmwʌn ə rʌn fə ðeə ˈmʌni/
vocab.meaning: tốt/đẹp không kém gì ai đó (thường nói đùa về sự tự tin)
vocab.example: When I'm fully dressed up, I like to think my reflection could give a movie star a run for their money.

vocab.word: at the drop of a hat
vocab.meaning: ngay lập tức/bất cứ khi nào có cơ hội
vocab.example: My younger sister is quite vain; she'll stop to check herself in any reflective surface at the drop of a hat.

vocab.meaning: người/vật được yêu quý nhất (dùng khi nói về việc tự yêu bản thân)
vocab.example: I don't look in the mirror because I think I'm the apple of my own eye; it’s purely out of a need for basic tidiness.

vocab.meaning: bằng xương bằng thịt/ngoài đời thực
vocab.example: Sometimes I’m surprised by how different I look in the mirror compared to photos—it’s the only way to see myself in the flesh, so to speak.
